findings.summary: GPU solver code (gpu_beam_search.py, gpu_sequential_solver.py, live_gpu_stream.py) has zero unit tests
evidence: gpu_beam_search.py (2266 lines) has no test file
gpu_sequential_solver.py (1450 lines) has no test file
live_gpu_stream.py (2388 lines) has no test file
CPU-testable functions like cpu_verify() and pre_simulate_locked() remain untested
findings.suggestion: Add CPU-only tests for cpu_verify(), cpu_verify_detailed(), pre_simulate_locked(), SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks construction. Mock torch.cuda for GPU-dependent paths.

findings.summary: At least 15 Python files are stale solver generations without DEPRECATED markers
evidence: beam_search.py, optimizer.py, seq_solver.py lack DEPRECATED markers
gpu_optimizer.py and gpu_sim.py use Numba CUDA superseded by PyTorch
Only planner_v2.py and planner_v3.py have DEPRECATED notices out of 15+ stale files
findings.suggestion: Add DEPRECATED module-level docstrings to all stale solver files.

findings.summary: refine_from_solution uses raw kwargs while solve_sequential migrated to SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks
evidence: solve_sequential() accepts SolveConfig and SolveCallbacks dataclass objects
refine_from_solution() uses 15 individual keyword arguments with no config object support
Docstring says 'Other args same as solve_sequential' but interface differs
findings.suggestion: Add SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks support to refine_from_solution() with the same legacy kwargs bridge.

findings.summary: Difficulty detection logic duplicated in JS endpoints and Python configs.py
findings.suggestion: Have Python scripts emit difficulty in JSON output to eliminate JS reimplementation.

findings.summary: GPU_DIR path resolution differs between iterate endpoint and other SvelteKit endpoints
evidence: iterate/+server.js uses 8 levels of '..' + direct child for GPU_DIR
All 6 other endpoints use 7 levels of '..' + '../grocery-bot-gpu'
Both resolve correctly but pattern is inconsistent
findings.suggestion: Standardize GPU_DIR resolution across all SvelteKit endpoints or extract to shared config.
